utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format lists 5 items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, and total_budget. The final coverage plan includes all 5 items with detailed specification. Each section contains the required details: health_coverage covers both physical and mental health with costs and coverage limit; property_coverage includes home/contents insurance details with costs; travel_coverage includes business and personal travel coverage with costs; additional_services describes enhancement services with costs; total_budget provides a complete monthly and annual cost breakdown. Therefore, all 5 required items are fully covered.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan lists specific selections for each coverage type. For health coverage (physical_health), it mentions 'PrimeCare Premium Plan with global coverage' — this aligns exactly with the 'PrimeCare Premium Plan' in the ground-truth ratings under primary_health_coverage → physical_health, which has a rating of 10. For mental_health, it also mentions 'Executive stress management programs... mental health coverage' under PrimeCare Premium Plan, corresponding to the same plan in primary_health_coverage → mental_health with a rating of 10. For property_coverage, the description 'Luxury items covered, business equipment covered, liability protection, temporary accommodation' matches the premium property protection level, most consistent with 'PrimeHome Premium Coverage' (rating 10). For travel_coverage, the 'business travel protection' and 'global assistance' indicate a premium global travel insurance, corresponding to 'PrimeTravel Premium Plan' (rating 10). Additional services include 'Dedicated agent, 24/7 customer support, global assistance, concierge services', which strongly aligns with 'Premium Insurance Membership' (rating 10) and potentially includes '24/7 Travel Assistance' (rating 10) as part of the support structure. Thus, each chosen component maps to its top-rated option.
